title: ChangeRepository Port
description: Without an abstraction over change storage, use cases would couple directly to filesystem layout and I/O, making them untestable and locked to a single storage strategy. `ChangeRepository` is the application-layer port for reading and writing changes, extending the shared `Repository` base class for interface consistency while operating globally — changes live in a single `changes/` directory, not per-workspace. This spec defines the contract that all implementations must satisfy.
context: Without an abstraction over change storage, use cases would couple directly to filesystem layout and I/O, making them untestable and locked to a single storage strategy. `ChangeRepository` is the application-layer port for reading and writing changes, extending the shared `Repository` base class for interface consistency while operating globally — changes live in a single `changes/` directory, not per-workspace. This spec defines the contract that all implementations must satisfy.

rules.requirement: Inheritance from Repository base
rules: `ChangeRepository` MUST extend `Repository`. The inherited `workspace()`, `ownership()`, and `isExternal()` accessors MUST be present but carry default values — no use case relies on them for `ChangeRepository`. They exist solely to satisfy the shared base contract.

rules.requirement: get returns a Change or null
rules:
`get(name)` MUST resolve the change name **only** under active storage (`changes/`). It MUST NOT search `drafts/` or `discarded/`.

When a change with the given name exists only as drafted, `get(name)` MUST return `null`.

When a change exists in active storage, `get(name)` MUST return the `Change` with artifact and file states loaded from the persisted manifest. If an artifact or file entry omits `state`, the repository defaults that missing value to `missing` while loading.

`validatedHash` is still loaded with the artifact data, but hash comparison is not the sole source of truth for steady-state status. The repository MAY detect drift and persist updated file and artifact states before returning (see Requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift).

`get()` is a snapshot read for **active** changes. It MAY auto-invalidate and persist drifted artifacts before returning, but it MUST NOT be the repository's serialized mutation primitive. Callers that need a coordinated read-modify-write section for an existing active change MUST use `mutate(name, fn)` instead of relying on a later `save()` against a stale snapshot.

rules.requirement: getDraft returns a DraftedChangeView or null
rules:
`getDraft(name)` MUST resolve the change name **only** under drafted storage (`drafts/`). It MUST NOT search `changes/` or `discarded/`.

When a drafted change exists, `getDraft(name)` MUST return a `DraftedChangeView` constructed from the persisted manifest. The view MUST satisfy [`core:drafted-change-view`](../drafted-change-view/spec.md).

When no drafted change exists with the given name, `getDraft(name)` MUST return `null`.

`getDraft()` MUST NOT auto-invalidate or persist manifest updates. Drift detection for drafted changes is out of scope for the active working set until the change is restored.

rules.requirement: getDiscarded returns a DiscardedChangeView or null
rules:
`getDiscarded(name)` MUST resolve the change name **only** under discarded storage (`discarded/`). It MUST NOT search `changes/` or `drafts/`.

When a discarded change exists, `getDiscarded(name)` MUST return a `DiscardedChangeView` built via `toDiscardedChangeView` from the shared inspection facade ([`core:read-only-change-view`](../read-only-change-view/spec.md)).

When no discarded change exists with the given name, `getDiscarded(name)` MUST return `null`.

`getDiscarded()` MUST NOT auto-invalidate or persist manifest updates.

rules.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
rules:
`mutate(name, fn)` MUST provide serialized read-modify-write semantics for one existing **active** persisted change.

Resolution MUST use the same rules as `get(name)` — only `changes/`. If the name exists only under `drafts/`, `mutate()` MUST throw `ChangeNotFoundError`.

For a given active change name, the repository MUST:

1. Acquire exclusive mutation access scoped to that persisted change
2. Reload the freshest persisted `Change` state from active storage after the exclusive access is acquired
3. Invoke `fn(change)` with that fresh `Change` where `change.isDrafted === false`
4. Persist the updated change manifest if `fn` resolves successfully
5. Release the exclusive access before returning or throwing

If no active change with the given name exists, `mutate()` MUST throw `ChangeNotFoundError`.

If `fn` throws, `mutate()` MUST release the exclusive access and MUST NOT persist a partial manifest update produced by the failed callback.

The serialized section MUST cover the full persisted mutation window — fresh load, callback execution, and manifest persistence. Locking only the final manifest write is insufficient.

Exclusive access is per change name, not global. Mutations targeting different change names MAY proceed concurrently.

rules.requirement: mutateDraft serializes drafted change updates
rules:
`mutateDraft(name, fn)` MUST provide serialized read-modify-write semantics for one existing **drafted** persisted change.

Resolution MUST use the same rules as `getDraft(name)` — only `drafts/`. If the name exists only under active storage, `mutateDraft()` MUST throw `ChangeNotFoundError`.

The callback MUST receive a fresh mutable `Change` with `isDrafted === true` before any transforming operation in the callback. Only `RestoreChange` and `DiscardChange` (and repository internals) MAY call `mutateDraft` in production code.

On success, the repository MUST persist the manifest and perform any required directory move (`drafts/` ↔ `changes/` or `drafts/` → `discarded/`).

If `fn` throws, `mutateDraft()` MUST NOT persist partial updates, matching `mutate` failure semantics.

rules.requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift
rules:
The `FsChangeRepository` implementation of `get()` MUST detect artifact file drift and auto-invalidate the change when appropriate. After loading a change, the repository compares the current cleaned file hash against each file's stored `validatedHash` for files that were previously validated.

A file is drifted when:

- `validatedHash` is a SHA-256 value recorded by prior validation, and
- the current cleaned content hash no longer matches that `validatedHash`

When drift is detected, the repository MUST:

1. Scan the full affected artifact set first, collecting every drifted file key grouped by artifact type. It MUST NOT stop at the first mismatch.
2. Mark each drifted file as `drifted-pending-review`.
3. Recompute every affected artifact's aggregate `state`.
4. Invalidate the change back to `designing` using the domain invalidation mechanism, preserving `drifted-pending-review` on the drifted files and downgrading the remaining files to `pending-review`.
5. Persist the updated manifest before returning.

This invalidation is lifecycle-independent: if a validated file drifts, the change is invalidated back to `designing` regardless of whether the current lifecycle state is `designing`, `ready`, `implementing`, `verifying`, `done`, or `archivable`.

The invalidation history entry MUST record:

- `cause: "artifact-drift"`
- a clear `message`
- `affectedArtifacts`, including each affected artifact type and the full list of drifted file keys captured in step 1

The `SYSTEM_ACTOR` constant (`{ name: 'specd', email: 'system@getspecd.dev' }`) is used as the actor for these automated invalidations.

rules.requirement: list returns active changes in creation order
rules: `list()` MUST return all active (non-drafted, non-discarded) changes sorted by creation order, oldest first. Each returned `Change` MUST include artifact state (derived statuses) but MUST NOT include artifact content. Content is loaded on demand via `artifact()`.

rules.requirement: listDrafts returns drafted changes in creation order
rules: `listDrafts()` MUST return all drafted changes sorted by creation order, oldest first. Each entry MUST be a `DraftedChangeView` with artifact state (derived statuses) but MUST NOT include artifact content. The repository MUST NOT return mutable `Change` instances to callers.

rules.requirement: listDiscarded returns discarded changes in creation order
rules: `listDiscarded()` MUST return all discarded changes sorted by creation order, oldest first. Each entry MUST be a `DiscardedChangeView` built via the shared inspection facade. The repository MUST NOT return mutable `Change` instances to callers.

rules.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
rules:
`save(change)` MUST persist the change manifest — state, artifact statuses, validated hashes, history events, and approvals. It MUST NOT write artifact file content. Artifact content is written exclusively via `saveArtifact()`. The write MUST be atomic (e.g. temp file + rename) to prevent partial reads.

If `change.isDrafted === true`, `save(change)` MUST throw `DraftedChangeReadOnlyError` unless the call originates from the serialized `mutateDraft` window for that change name.

`save()` is a low-level persistence operation. Atomic manifest writing prevents partial-file corruption, but `save()` alone does not serialize a caller's earlier snapshot read. Use cases that mutate an existing active persisted change and need concurrency safety MUST perform that mutation through `mutate(name, fn)`.

rules.requirement: delete removes the entire change directory
rules: `delete(change)` MUST remove the entire change directory and all its contents from the filesystem.

rules.requirement: artifact loads content with originalHash
rules: `artifact(change, filename)` MUST load the content of a single artifact file within a change and return a `SpecArtifact`. The returned artifact's `originalHash` MUST be set to the `sha256` hash of the content read from disk, enabling conflict detection on subsequent saves. If the file does not exist, the method MUST return `null`.

rules.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
rules:
`saveArtifact(change, artifact, options?)` MUST write an artifact file within a change directory. If `change.isDrafted === true`, `saveArtifact` MUST throw `DraftedChangeReadOnlyError` before any filesystem write.

If `artifact.originalHash` is set, the implementation MUST compare it against the current hash of the file on disk before writing. If the hashes differ, the save MUST be rejected by throwing `ArtifactConflictError` — this prevents silently overwriting concurrent modifications. When `options.force` is `true`, the conflict check MUST be skipped and the file MUST be overwritten unconditionally. After a successful write, the corresponding `ChangeArtifact` status in the change manifest MUST be reset to `in-progress`; the caller is responsible for calling `save(change)` to persist this state change.

rules.requirement: artifactExists checks file presence without loading
rules: `artifactExists(change, filename)` MUST return `true` if the artifact file exists within the change directory, `false` otherwise. It MUST NOT load the file content.

rules.requirement: deltaExists checks delta file presence
rules: `deltaExists(change, specId, filename)` MUST return `true` if the specified delta file exists for the given change and spec ID, `false` otherwise. Delta files are located within a subdirectory of the change directory identified by the spec ID.

rules.requirement: changePath returns the absolute path to a change directory
rules: `changePath(change)` MUST accept a `Change` and return the absolute filesystem path to that change's directory. This is used by use cases that need the change path for template variable construction (e.g. `change.path` in `TemplateVariables`). The implementation resolves the path from its internal storage layout.

rules.requirement: draftChangePath returns the drafted directory path
rules:
`draftChangePath(view)` MUST accept a `DraftedChangeView` (or the internal drafted `Change` during `mutateDraft`) and return the absolute filesystem path under `drafts/` for template variables and CLI display.

`changePath(change)` MUST continue to resolve paths under `changes/` for active changes only.

rules.requirement: scaffold creates artifact directories
rules:
`scaffold(change, specExists)` MUST create the directory structure needed for the change's
artifacts. For `scope: spec` artifacts, it creates `specs/<ws>/<capPath>/` or
`deltas/<ws>/<capPath>/` directories under the change directory. For `scope: change`
artifacts, the root directory already exists. The `specExists` callback is an async function
that returns whether a spec already exists in the repository, used to determine whether
to create spec-scoped or delta-scoped directories.

rules.requirement: unscaffold removes spec directories
rules:
`unscaffold(change, specIds)` MUST remove the scaffolded directories for the given
spec IDs from the change directory. For each spec ID, it MUST remove:

- `specs/<workspace>/<capability-path>/` — new-spec artifact directories
- `deltas/<workspace>/<capability-path>/` — delta artifact directories

The operation MUST be idempotent — if a directory does not exist, it MUST be silently
skipped. If a directory contains files, all files and subdirectories MUST be removed
along with the directory itself.

rules.requirement: Abstract class with abstract methods
rules: `ChangeRepository` MUST be defined as an `abstract class`, not an `interface`. All storage operations (`get`, `getDraft`, `getDiscarded`, `mutate`, `mutateDraft`, `list`, `listDrafts`, `listDiscarded`, `save`, `delete`, `artifact`, `saveArtifact`, `artifactExists`, `deltaExists`, `changePath`, `draftChangePath`, `scaffold`, `unscaffold`) MUST be declared as `abstract` methods. This follows the architecture spec requirement that ports with shared construction are abstract classes.

rules.requirement: artifact only loads tracked change artifact files
rules:
`artifact(change, filename)` MUST accept only filenames that correspond to tracked artifact files declared on the change's artifact file list.

If `filename` does not match a tracked artifact file for that change, the repository MUST reject the read rather than treating the change directory as a general-purpose file container.

rules.requirement: Change artifact path confinement
rules:
`artifact(change, filename)`, `artifactExists(change, filename)`, and any related change-artifact file lookup MUST enforce strict confinement to the change directory.

The repository MUST reject any path that would escape the change directory or address an arbitrary file outside the tracked artifact set, including path traversal forms and alternate relative-path encodings.

rules.requirement: Change artifact resolution debug logging
rules:
Implementations SHOULD emit debug-level logs when resolving tracked change artifact files, rejecting untracked filenames, or rejecting a path-confinement violation.

These logs MUST follow the project's global logging conventions.

constraints: Changes are stored globally, not per-workspace — the inherited workspace context is unused
get() in FsChangeRepository may auto-invalidate and persist the change before returning, if artifact drift is detected and the change is beyond designing or has active approvals
All list methods return Change objects with derived artifact state but without artifact content
save() writes the manifest only; saveArtifact() writes file content only — these are separate operations
ArtifactConflictError is the sole error type for concurrent modification detection
The force option on saveArtifact() bypasses conflict detection entirely
originalHash on loaded artifacts MUST use sha256 of the file content as read from disk
Manifest writes MUST be atomic to prevent corruption from partial reads

scenarios.requirement: get returns a Change or null
scenarios.name: Active change exists
when: get("add-oauth-login") is called and a change with that name exists under changes/
then: a Change is returned with persisted artifact and file state loaded from the manifest

scenarios.requirement: get returns a Change or null
scenarios.name: Drafted-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under drafts/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: get returns a Change or null
scenarios.name: Discarded-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under discarded/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: get returns a Change or null
scenarios.name: Missing state defaults to missing on load
given: a manifest entry without a state field
then: the missing artifact or file state defaults to missing

scenarios.requirement: get returns a Change or null
scenarios.name: get returns a snapshot, not a serialized mutation context
given: two callers both loaded the same active change via get()
when: each caller mutates its in-memory instance and only one uses mutate()
then: the unsynchronized caller must not rely on save() alone for a safe persisted update

scenarios.requirement: getDraft returns a DraftedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Drafted change returns view
when: getDraft(name) is called and the change exists under drafts/
then: a DraftedChangeView is returned with isDrafted === true

scenarios.requirement: getDraft returns a DraftedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Active-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under changes/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: getDraft returns a DraftedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Discarded-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under discarded/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: getDraft returns a DraftedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: View has no artifact file bodies
given: a drafted change with on-disk proposal.md
then: the view exposes artifact statuses without embedding file content

scenarios.requirement: getDiscarded returns a DiscardedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Discarded change returns view
when: getDiscarded(name) is called and the change exists under discarded/
then: a DiscardedChangeView is returned with discardReason from the latest discarded event

scenarios.requirement: getDiscarded returns a DiscardedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Active-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under changes/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: getDiscarded returns a DiscardedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: Drafted-only name returns null
given: a change exists only under drafts/
then: it returns null

scenarios.requirement: getDiscarded returns a DiscardedChangeView or null
scenarios.name: View exposes supersededBy when present
given: a discarded change whose discarded event includes supersededBy: ['replacement']
then: the returned view's supersededBy equals ['replacement']

scenarios.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
scenarios.name: Missing active change is rejected
when: mutate("missing-change", fn) is called and no active change with that name exists
then: ChangeNotFoundError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
scenarios.name: Drafted-only name is rejected
given: a change exists only under drafts/
then: ChangeNotFoundError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
scenarios.name: Discarded-only name is rejected
given: a change exists only under discarded/
then: ChangeNotFoundError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
scenarios.name: Second mutation sees the first mutation's persisted result
given: two callers both request mutate() for the same active change name
AND the first callback appends a history event and resolves
then: it receives a freshly reloaded Change that already includes the first callback's persisted update

scenarios.requirement: mutate serializes persisted change updates
scenarios.name: Failing callback does not persist a partial manifest update
when: mutate(name, fn) is called and fn throws after mutating the in-memory change
then: the on-disk manifest is unchanged from before the mutate call began

scenarios.requirement: mutateDraft serializes drafted change updates
scenarios.name: Restore uses mutateDraft
then: ChangeRepository.mutateDraft was used for that name

scenarios.requirement: mutateDraft serializes drafted change updates
scenarios.name: Active name is rejected
given: a change exists only under changes/
then: ChangeNotFoundError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: mutateDraft serializes drafted change updates
scenarios.name: Discarded name is rejected
given: a change exists only under discarded/
then: ChangeNotFoundError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: mutateDraft serializes drafted change updates
scenarios.name: Callback receives fresh drafted Change
given: mutateDraft(name, fn) is invoked for a drafted change
then: it receives a freshly loaded Change with isDrafted === true

scenarios.requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift
scenarios.name: Repository collects all drifted files before invalidating
given: a change with two validated spec files under the same artifact
AND both files have changed on disk
then: the invalidation captures both file keys in a single grouped invalidation

scenarios.requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift
scenarios.name: Drift invalidates even while already designing
given: a change already in designing
AND a previously validated artifact file drifts on disk
then: the change remains in designing
AND the drifted file becomes drifted-pending-review

scenarios.requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift
scenarios.name: Drift preserves drifted files and downgrades others to pending review
given: a change with validated artifacts
AND one file drifts on disk
then: the drifted file is drifted-pending-review
AND other previously validated files become pending-review

scenarios.requirement: Auto-invalidation on get when artifact files drift
scenarios.name: No drift — no invalidation
given: a change whose validated files still match their stored hashes
then: no invalidation occurs

scenarios.requirement: list returns active changes in creation order
scenarios.name: Mixed active and drafted changes
given: three changes exist: one active (created first), one drafted, one active (created last)
then: only the two active changes are returned, ordered oldest first

scenarios.requirement: list returns active changes in creation order
scenarios.name: No active changes
when: list() is called and no active changes exist
then: an empty array is returned

scenarios.requirement: listDrafts returns drafted changes in creation order
scenarios.name: Only drafted changes returned
given: two drafted changes and one active change exist
then: only the two drafted changes are returned, ordered oldest first

scenarios.requirement: listDiscarded returns discarded changes in creation order
scenarios.name: Only discarded changes returned
given: one discarded change and two active changes exist
then: only the discarded change is returned

scenarios.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
scenarios.name: Save does not write artifact content
given: a change with modified artifact content
then: the manifest file is updated with current state, hashes, and history
AND artifact file content on disk is unchanged

scenarios.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
scenarios.name: Save alone does not serialize an earlier snapshot read
given: a caller holds a Change loaded earlier via get()
when: another caller persists newer manifest state before save(oldSnapshot) runs
then: save() still behaves as a low-level manifest write
AND callers that need concurrency-safe read-modify-write behavior must use mutate()

scenarios.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
scenarios.name: save on drafted change outside mutateDraft throws
given: a persisted change with isDrafted === true
when: save(change) is called outside an active mutateDraft window
then: DraftedChangeReadOnlyError is thrown

scenarios.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
scenarios.name: save inside mutateDraft succeeds
given: mutateDraft(name, fn) is executing for a drafted change
when: the callback triggers an internal save(change) for that same name
then: DraftedChangeReadOnlyError is not thrown

scenarios.requirement: save persists the change manifest only
scenarios.name: save on active change is unchanged
given: a change exists under changes/ with isDrafted === false
then: persistence proceeds per existing optimistic concurrency rules

scenarios.requirement: artifact loads content with originalHash
scenarios.name: Artifact exists
given: a change with an artifact file proposal.md on disk
then: a SpecArtifact is returned with the file content and originalHash set to sha256 of that content

scenarios.requirement: artifact loads content with originalHash
scenarios.name: Artifact does not exist
then: null is returned

scenarios.requirement: artifact only loads tracked change artifact files
scenarios.name: Tracked artifact file can be read
given: proposal.md is listed in the change's tracked artifact files
then: the repository returns that tracked artifact content

scenarios.requirement: artifact only loads tracked change artifact files
scenarios.name: Untracked file is rejected even when present on disk
given: a file exists inside the change directory but is not listed in the change's tracked artifact files
then: the repository rejects the read instead of returning arbitrary content

scenarios.requirement: Change artifact path confinement
scenarios.name: Path traversal is rejected on artifact read
when: artifact(change, "../outside.txt") or an equivalent escape path is requested
then: the repository rejects the request

scenarios.requirement: Change artifact path confinement
scenarios.name: Path traversal is rejected on existence check
when: artifactExists(change, "../outside.txt") or an equivalent escape path is requested
then: the repository rejects the request

scenarios.requirement: Change artifact resolution debug logging
scenarios.name: Debug logs cover tracked resolution and rejection
when: debug logging is enabled for ChangeRepository
then: successful tracked artifact resolution emits debug output
AND untracked filename rejection or path-confinement rejection also emits debug output

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: No conflict — originalHash matches
given: an artifact loaded with originalHash and the file on disk has not changed
then: the file is written successfully

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: Conflict detected — originalHash mismatch
given: an artifact loaded with originalHash and the file on disk was modified by another process
when: saveArtifact(change, artifact) is called without force
then: ArtifactConflictError is thrown with filename, incomingContent, and currentContent

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: Force bypasses conflict detection
given: an artifact whose originalHash does not match the current file on disk
when: saveArtifact(change, artifact, { force: true }) is called
then: the file is overwritten without error

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: New artifact with no originalHash
given: an artifact with originalHash undefined (first write)
then: the file is written without conflict check

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: saveArtifact on drafted change throws
given: a change with isDrafted === true
when: saveArtifact(change, artifact) is called outside mutateDraft
then: DraftedChangeReadOnlyError is thrown before any filesystem write

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: saveArtifact inside mutateDraft may succeed
given: mutateDraft(name, fn) is executing for a drafted change
when: the callback calls saveArtifact for that change
then: DraftedChangeReadOnlyError is not thrown for the drafted guard alone

scenarios.requirement: saveArtifact with optimistic concurrency
scenarios.name: saveArtifact on active change is unchanged
given: a change under changes/ with isDrafted === false
when: saveArtifact(change, artifact) is called with a valid hash
then: existing optimistic concurrency behaviour applies

scenarios.requirement: artifactExists checks file presence without loading
scenarios.name: File exists
given: a change with artifact file tasks.md on disk
then: true is returned

scenarios.requirement: artifactExists checks file presence without loading
scenarios.name: File does not exist
then: false is returned

scenarios.requirement: deltaExists checks delta file presence
scenarios.name: Delta file exists
given: a change with delta file spec.delta.yaml for spec ID auth/login
then: true is returned

scenarios.requirement: deltaExists checks delta file presence
scenarios.name: Delta file does not exist
then: false is returned

scenarios.requirement: unscaffold removes spec directories
scenarios.name: Unscaffold removes specs and deltas directories
given: a change directory with specs/core/core/edit-change/ and deltas/core/core/edit-change/ subdirectories
then: both specs/core/core/edit-change/ and deltas/core/core/edit-change/ directories are removed

scenarios.requirement: unscaffold removes spec directories
scenarios.name: Unscaffold is idempotent — non-existent directory is silently skipped
given: a change directory with no specs/core/core/edit-change/ directory
then: no error is thrown
AND the operation completes successfully

scenarios.requirement: unscaffold removes spec directories
scenarios.name: Unscaffold removes directories with files
given: a change directory with specs/core/core/edit-change/spec.md (a file inside the directory)
then: the specs/core/core/edit-change/ directory and its contents are removed

scenarios.requirement: Inheritance from Repository base
scenarios.name: Repository extends Repository base class
when: ChangeRepository is examined in the codebase
then: it extends a base Repository class

scenarios.requirement: delete removes the entire change directory
scenarios.name: Delete removes change directory
given: a change in the changes directory with manifest and artifact files
then: the entire change directory is removed

scenarios.requirement: changePath returns the absolute path to a change directory
scenarios.name: changePath resolves to absolute path
given: a change with name "my-change"
then: it returns the absolute path to the change directory

scenarios.requirement: draftChangePath returns the drafted directory path
scenarios.name: Path resolves under drafts
given: a DraftedChangeView for change parked-feature
then: the returned path is under the configured drafts/ directory

scenarios.requirement: draftChangePath returns the drafted directory path
scenarios.name: Path ends with change name segment
given: a DraftedChangeView with name: 'parked-feature'
then: the returned path basename is parked-feature

scenarios.requirement: draftChangePath returns the drafted directory path
scenarios.name: Active changePath is not used for drafted view
given: a DraftedChangeView for a drafted change
when: resolving filesystem location for inspection tooling
then: callers use draftChangePath(view) rather than changePath with an active Change

scenarios.requirement: scaffold creates artifact directories
scenarios.name: Scaffold creates spec directories
given: a change with specIds including core:edit-change
then: it creates the specs/core/core/edit-change/ directory

scenarios.requirement: Abstract class with abstract methods
scenarios.name: ChangeRepository declares abstract methods
then: it is an abstract class with abstract methods for get, list, save, mutate
